untranslatable_reason: Bearish Belt Hold is a specific candlestick pattern from Table 12.1 whose exact identification criteria are not provided in the source excerpt. The pattern requires visual recognition of: (1) a large white candle at resistance in an uptrend, (2) a black candle opening at or near the prior close, and (3) closing in the lower half of the day's range. These precise morphological requirements cannot be fully expressed using only the allowed technical indicators. The conditions provided (close below previous close, close below upper Bollinger Band) are approximations but do not capture the complete pattern definition.
translation_notes: Attempted translation using price action signals: entry when price closes below prior close and below upper Bollinger Band (suggesting rejection at resistance); exit when price crosses back above SMA_20 (trend reversal). However, this is a simplified proxy lacking the specific candlestick body/wick ratio analysis required for authentic Bearish Belt Hold identification.
